def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    window = params['window']
    atr_multiplier = params['atr_multiplier']
    rsi_threshold = params['rsi_threshold']
    
    df['high_band'] = df['high'].rolling(window).max()
    df['low_band'] = df['low'].rolling(window).min()
    df['atr'] = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=window).average_true_range()
    df['upper_band'] = df['high_band'] + (df['atr'] * atr_multiplier)
    df['lower_band'] = df['low_band'] - (df['atr'] * atr_multiplier)
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.RSIIndicator(df['close'], window=window).rsi()
    
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signals[(df['close'] > df['upper_band']) & (df['rsi'] > rsi_threshold)] = -1
    signals[(df['close'] < df['lower_band']) & (df['rsi'] < (100 - rsi_threshold))] = 1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/JPY exhibits strong support/resistance levels where price tends to reverse after breaching dynamic bands adjusted by ATR and confirmed by RSI overbought/oversold conditions.
